assumptions: The developer may create an Adobe ID, but no Experience Platform organization entitlement, Admin Console role, sandbox, or API credential exists yet for that developer.
No Experience Platform API surface beyond generic authentication has been preselected.
The organization may or may not already be licensed for Experience Platform.

developer_goal: Obtain authorized Adobe Experience Platform API credentials and prove them with the earliest official authenticated API result.

first_success.outcome: Generate Experience Platform API credentials and successfully list standard global XDM classes from the Schema Registry.
first_success.observable_signal: GET https://platform.adobe.io/data/foundation/schemaregistry/global/classes returns standard XDM classes using the generated access token, API key, and org ID.

route_selection.surface: Adobe Developer Console and Experience Platform authentication tutorial
route_selection.rule: Use the official authentication tutorial's credential-test terminal only when organization entitlement and Admin Console grants are available. Do not treat unlicensed self-serve signup as equivalent.

candidates.name: Licensed org: Admin Console developer+user grants, Developer Console OAuth Server-to-Server, Schema Registry global classes test
candidates.reason: Official API authentication path, but it presupposes an organization with Experience Platform access and an Admin Console administrator.

candidates.name: API reference page Sign in and generate credentials in-docs
candidates.reason: Documented alternate credential collection UX that still requires the same org entitlement and permissions.

candidates.name: Unaffiliated Adobe ID with no Experience Platform organization
candidates.reason: Public evidence does not establish a self-serve path to a licensed Experience Platform org from account creation alone.

required_path.action: Create or sign in with an Adobe ID.

required_fields.label: Adobe ID signup or sign-in fields
required_fields.notes: Tutorial allows Create a new account but does not enumerate signup fields.

required_path.observable_result: The developer can authenticate to Adobe Developer Console.

required_path.action: Have an Admin Console administrator add the developer as both developer and user on an Experience Platform product profile.
required_path.observable_result: The Adobe ID can create Experience Platform API integrations for the organization.

required_path.action: Create a Developer Console project, add Experience Platform API with OAuth Server-to-Server, and select product profiles.
required_path.observable_result: API_KEY and ORG_ID credentials are available on the project.

required_path.action: Generate an access token and execute the documented Schema Registry global classes request.
required_path.observable_result: The global classes endpoint returns standard XDM classes.

prerequisites.description: An organization with access to Adobe Experience Platform is required.
An Admin Console administrator must be able to add the developer and user product-profile grants.
A system administrator may also need to grant attribute-based access controls for API operations.

external_gates.description: Experience Platform licensing and organization membership sit outside self-serve Adobe ID creation.
Admin Console administrator action is required before Developer Console integrations can be created.
Only system administrators can add developers and assign API credential permissions in the documented admin flow.

unavoidable_waits.description: Administrator grant processing and any organization invitation or procurement steps must complete before API project setup.
Access tokens must be refreshed about every 24 hours after initial generation.

platform_outcomes.description: Developer Console issues API_KEY, ORG_ID, and access tokens for OAuth Server-to-Server credentials.
Schema Registry returns global XDM classes for a successful authenticated test call.

excluded.item: Post-authentication business workflows such as ingestion, profile, or segmentation
excluded.reason: The authentication tutorial intentionally stops at a generic credential test.

excluded.item: Reading sandboxes conceptual documentation beyond the auth tutorial headers
excluded.reason: Sandbox selection is a later API concern once credentials exist.

sources.title: Authenticate and access Experience Platform APIs
Add developers and give permissions to API credentials
Sandboxes in Experience Platform
evidence_supported: Sandbox isolation context for later API calls

uncertainties.question: Can a developer starting only with account creation obtain a licensed Experience Platform organization without prior enterprise procurement or invitation?
uncertainties.impact: If not, the journey is blocked by entitlement before any Developer Console step, and counts cannot be verified from public self-serve evidence.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Official public documentation of a self-serve Experience Platform org path, or confirmation that enterprise entitlement is always required.

uncertainties.question: What exact Adobe ID signup fields and organization-invitation steps precede Admin Console access for a cold developer?
uncertainties.impact: Account-creation and invitation burden remain unverified.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: A dated walkthrough of Adobe ID creation plus any org invite or Admin Console first-access flow.

uncertainties.question: Which Experience Platform API workflow should follow generic authentication as the organization-wide first mile?
uncertainties.impact: Schema, ingestion, profile, and other APIs add different roles, sandbox headers, and success signals after the credential test.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: A bounded API goal beyond the global XDM classes credential test.
